points.text: COVID-19 is a contagious disease caused by the coronavirus SARS-CoV-2.
The disease spread worldwide starting in January 2020, leading to a pandemic.
WHO declared COVID-19 a global health emergency in March 2020 and ended it in May 2023.
COVID-19 is shorthand for 'coronavirus disease 2019', named by WHO on 11 February 2020.
COVID-19 can be diagnosed with RT-PCR or other nucleic acid tests and confirmed with chest CT scans.
COVID-19 is an illness caused by the virus SARS-CoV-2, which began spreading in late 2019 and became a pandemic in 2020.
The virus spreads mainly through the air via tiny droplets from coughs, sneezes, or talking, especially in close contact.
COVID-19 can cause mild illness or no symptoms, but can lead to hospitalization or death in older adults and those with certain conditions.
It's possible to get COVID-19 more than once due to fading immunity or virus mutations.
Preventive measures include vaccination, good airflow, physical distancing, mask-wearing, and hygiene.
COVID-19 is an infectious respiratory illness caused by the coronavirus SARS-CoV-2.
Symptoms of COVID-19 can appear 2 to 14 days after exposure and range from mild to severe.
Urgent symptoms include difficulty breathing, blue lips, chest pain, confusion, and severe stomach pain.
COVID-19 vaccines undergo rigorous testing to meet safety and effectiveness standards.
Precautions to prevent COVID-19 include vaccination, physical distancing, mask wearing, and handwashing.
